questions.q: What kind of creature is Jatayu in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is depicted as a mighty vulture, the noble king among birds of his kind.

questions.q: Jatayu was a friend of which king, the father of Rama?
questions.explanation: Jatayu was an old friend of King Dasharatha, Rama's father.

questions.q: Whom did Jatayu try to rescue from being carried away?
questions.explanation: Jatayu fought to save Sita as she was being abducted.

questions.q: Who was the demon king abducting Sita when Jatayu intervened?
questions.explanation: Ravana, king of Lanka, was carrying Sita away when Jatayu confronted him.

questions.q: In what was Ravana flying when he carried off Sita?
questions.explanation: Ravana abducted Sita in his flying aerial chariot.

questions.q: What did Jatayu ultimately give in his attempt to save Sita?
questions.explanation: Jatayu sacrificed his life in the battle to rescue Sita.

questions.q: How is Jatayu described in age?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is portrayed as an aged king of vultures.

questions.q: Jatayu is often called the king of which birds?
questions.explanation: Jatayu is honored as the noble king of vultures.

questions.q: To whom did the dying Jatayu reveal the direction Ravana had gone?
questions.explanation: The mortally wounded Jatayu told Rama and Lakshmana about Ravana before dying.

questions.q: What was Jatayu's main motive for attacking Ravana?
options: To protect Sita out of duty and friendship
questions.explanation: Out of loyalty to Dasharatha and righteousness, Jatayu fought to protect Sita.

questions.q: Who was Jatayu's brother, also a great bird in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Sampati was Jatayu's elder brother, another mighty bird.

questions.q: How did Jatayu and Sampati's father connect them to celestial lineage?
options: They were sons of Aruna (Garuda's brother)
They were sons of Surya himself
questions.explanation: In tradition Jatayu and Sampati are sons of Aruna, the charioteer of the Sun and brother of Garuda.

questions.q: When Jatayu first heard Sita's cries, what did he do?
options: Roused himself and challenged Ravana to release her
questions.explanation: Jatayu awoke, confronted Ravana, and demanded he free Sita.

questions.q: During the fight, what damage did Jatayu inflict on Ravana's chariot?
options: He destroyed the chariot, killed the mules and the charioteer
questions.explanation: Jatayu wrecked Ravana's chariot, slew its donkey-like steeds and the charioteer before being struck down.

questions.q: How did Ravana finally overcome Jatayu?
options: He fled and abandoned the fight
He cut off Jatayu's wings with his sword
He drowned him in the sea
questions.explanation: Ravana severed Jatayu's wings, sides, and feet with his sword, mortally wounding him.

questions.q: After Jatayu died, what did Rama and Lakshmana do for him?
options: Left him for the forest beasts
Performed his funeral rites and cremation
questions.explanation: Rama performed the last rites and cremation for Jatayu as for a beloved kinsman.

questions.q: Rama treated the dying Jatayu with the affection due to whom?
questions.explanation: Rama regarded Jatayu, his father's friend, with the love owed to a father figure.

questions.q: What crucial information did Jatayu give before dying that aided the search for Sita?
options: The recipe to break Ravana's spell
That Ravana, son of Vishrava, had taken her southward
The location of the ocean bridge
The name of Sita's future rescuer
questions.explanation: Jatayu named Ravana and indicated the direction of the abduction, guiding Rama's search.

questions.q: Jatayu's role in the Ramayana is most associated with which virtue?
questions.explanation: Jatayu exemplifies selfless sacrifice and unwavering loyalty to dharma.

questions.q: In which forest region did the encounter between Jatayu and Ravana take place?
questions.explanation: Sita was abducted from near Panchavati in the Dandaka forest, where Jatayu intervened.

questions.q: What had Jatayu promised Dasharatha regarding Rama and his family?
options: To watch over and protect them in the forest
To rule Ayodhya in his absence
questions.explanation: Out of friendship, Jatayu had pledged to look after Rama, Lakshmana, and Sita.

questions.q: Why was Jatayu, despite his great age, willing to fight a powerful demon king?
options: His sense of duty and righteousness compelled him
He was forced by a curse
questions.explanation: His commitment to dharma and his bond with Dasharatha's family drove him to fight.

questions.q: According to tradition, Jatayu's brother Sampati lost what in his youth?
options: The feathers of his wings (scorched by the sun)
questions.explanation: Sampati's wings were burned when he shielded Jatayu from the sun's heat, leaving him unable to fly.

questions.q: How did Sampati later help Rama's cause, continuing his brother's legacy?
options: He flew the army to Lanka
He revealed that Sita was held in Lanka across the sea
He built the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Sampati, on learning of Jatayu's death, told the searching monkeys that Sita was in Lanka.

questions.q: In the Aranya Kanda, what did Ravana first attempt before resorting to force against Jatayu?
options: To simply fly past and ignore the aged bird
questions.explanation: Ravana at first tried to evade the old vulture and continue his flight before the battle erupted.

questions.q: What did Jatayu warn Ravana about the consequences of abducting Sita?
options: That it would lead to Ravana's and Lanka's destruction
That Sita would curse him to stone
That the gods would reward him
questions.explanation: Jatayu warned that seizing another's wife unrighteously would doom Ravana and his kingdom.

questions.q: Which of Sita's belongings is associated with the abduction episode in which Jatayu fought?
options: Her ornaments, which she dropped as a trail
questions.explanation: Sita cast down her jewels during the abduction so they might mark Ravana's path, the same flight Jatayu opposed.

questions.q: What is the traditional name of the place said to be where Jatayu fell, now a pilgrimage site?
questions.explanation: Lepakshi in Andhra Pradesh is traditionally tied to Jatayu's fall, its name linked to Rama's words 'le pakshi' (rise, bird).

questions.q: In Valmiki's account, what blessing or assurance does the dying Jatayu receive from Rama?
options: That he would be reborn as a king
Words of grace, with Rama granting him liberation and an honorable departure
questions.explanation: Rama consoled Jatayu and granted him a gracious, liberated passing for his noble sacrifice.

questions.q: Jatayu's death and last words mark the transition into which phase of Rama's quest?
options: The active search for Sita leading toward Kishkindha and the vanaras
questions.explanation: Jatayu's revelation set Rama and Lakshmana on the southward search that led to Kishkindha and the monkey allies.
